# Proverbs 27

## Verses (27)

| # | Basic English Bible | Compare |
|---|---|---|
| [1](BBE.md#v1) | Do not make a noise about tomorrow, for you are not certain what a day's outcome may be. | [WEB](WEB.md#v1) · [KJV](KJV.md#v1) · [ASV](ASV.md#v1) |
| [2](BBE.md#v2) | Let another man give you praise, and not your mouth; one who is strange to you, and not your lips. | [WEB](WEB.md#v2) · [KJV](KJV.md#v2) · [ASV](ASV.md#v2) |
| [3](BBE.md#v3) | A stone has great weight, and sand is crushing; but the wrath of the foolish is of greater weight than these. | [WEB](WEB.md#v3) · [KJV](KJV.md#v3) · [ASV](ASV.md#v3) |
| [4](BBE.md#v4) | Wrath is cruel, and angry feeling an overflowing stream; but who does not give way before envy? | [WEB](WEB.md#v4) · [KJV](KJV.md#v4) · [ASV](ASV.md#v4) |
| [5](BBE.md#v5) | Better is open protest than love kept secret. | [WEB](WEB.md#v5) · [KJV](KJV.md#v5) · [ASV](ASV.md#v5) |
| [6](BBE.md#v6) | The wounds of a friend are given in good faith, but the kisses of a hater are false. | [WEB](WEB.md#v6) · [KJV](KJV.md#v6) · [ASV](ASV.md#v6) |
| [7](BBE.md#v7) | The full man has no use for honey, but to the man in need of food every bitter thing is sweet. | [WEB](WEB.md#v7) · [KJV](KJV.md#v7) · [ASV](ASV.md#v7) |
| [8](BBE.md#v8) | Like a bird wandering from the place of her eggs is a man wandering from his station. | [WEB](WEB.md#v8) · [KJV](KJV.md#v8) · [ASV](ASV.md#v8) |
| [9](BBE.md#v9) | Oil and perfume make glad the heart, and the wise suggestion of a friend is sweet to the soul. | [WEB](WEB.md#v9) · [KJV](KJV.md#v9) · [ASV](ASV.md#v9) |
| [10](BBE.md#v10) | Do not give up your friend and your father's friend; and do not go into your brother's house in the day of your trouble: better is a neighbour who is near than a brother far off. | [WEB](WEB.md#v10) · [KJV](KJV.md#v10) · [ASV](ASV.md#v10) |
| [11](BBE.md#v11) | My son, be wise and make my heart glad, so that I may give back an answer to him who puts me to shame. | [WEB](WEB.md#v11) · [KJV](KJV.md#v11) · [ASV](ASV.md#v11) |
| [12](BBE.md#v12) | The sharp man sees the evil and takes cover: the simple go straight on and get into trouble. | [WEB](WEB.md#v12) · [KJV](KJV.md#v12) · [ASV](ASV.md#v12) |
| [13](BBE.md#v13) | Take a man's clothing if he makes himself responsible for a strange man, and get an undertaking from him who gives his word for strange men. | [WEB](WEB.md#v13) · [KJV](KJV.md#v13) · [ASV](ASV.md#v13) |
| [14](BBE.md#v14) | He who gives a blessing to his friend with a loud voice, getting up early in the morning, will have it put to his account as a curse. | [WEB](WEB.md#v14) · [KJV](KJV.md#v14) · [ASV](ASV.md#v14) |
| [15](BBE.md#v15) | Like an unending dropping on a day of rain is a bitter-tongued woman. | [WEB](WEB.md#v15) · [KJV](KJV.md#v15) · [ASV](ASV.md#v15) |
| [16](BBE.md#v16) | He who keeps secret the secret of his friend, will get himself a name for good faith. | [WEB](WEB.md#v16) · [KJV](KJV.md#v16) · [ASV](ASV.md#v16) |
| [17](BBE.md#v17) | Iron makes iron sharp; so a man makes sharp his friend. | [WEB](WEB.md#v17) · [KJV](KJV.md#v17) · [ASV](ASV.md#v17) |
| [18](BBE.md#v18) | Whoever keeps a fig-tree will have its fruit; and the servant waiting on his master will be honoured. | [WEB](WEB.md#v18) · [KJV](KJV.md#v18) · [ASV](ASV.md#v18) |
| [19](BBE.md#v19) | Like face looking at face in water, so are the hearts of men to one another. | [WEB](WEB.md#v19) · [KJV](KJV.md#v19) · [ASV](ASV.md#v19) |
| [20](BBE.md#v20) | The underworld and Abaddon are never full, and the eyes of man have never enough. | [WEB](WEB.md#v20) · [KJV](KJV.md#v20) · [ASV](ASV.md#v20) |
| [21](BBE.md#v21) | The heating-pot is for silver and the oven-fire for gold, and a man is measured by what he is praised for. | [WEB](WEB.md#v21) · [KJV](KJV.md#v21) · [ASV](ASV.md#v21) |
| [22](BBE.md#v22) | Even if a foolish man is crushed with a hammer in a vessel among crushed grain, still his foolish ways will not go from him. | [WEB](WEB.md#v22) · [KJV](KJV.md#v22) · [ASV](ASV.md#v22) |
| [23](BBE.md#v23) | Take care to have knowledge about the condition of your flocks, looking well after your herds; | [WEB](WEB.md#v23) · [KJV](KJV.md#v23) · [ASV](ASV.md#v23) |
| [24](BBE.md#v24) | For wealth is not for ever, and money does not go on for all generations. | [WEB](WEB.md#v24) · [KJV](KJV.md#v24) · [ASV](ASV.md#v24) |
| [25](BBE.md#v25) | The grass comes up and the young grass is seen, and the mountain plants are got in. | [WEB](WEB.md#v25) · [KJV](KJV.md#v25) · [ASV](ASV.md#v25) |
| [26](BBE.md#v26) | The lambs are for your clothing, and the he-goats make the value of a field: | [WEB](WEB.md#v26) · [KJV](KJV.md#v26) · [ASV](ASV.md#v26) |
| [27](BBE.md#v27) | There will be goats' milk enough for your food, and for the support of your servant-girls. | [WEB](WEB.md#v27) · [KJV](KJV.md#v27) · [ASV](ASV.md#v27) |
